# The Walkthrough

> The Walkthrough is an iOS app where real estate agents practice high-stakes client
> conversations against an AI client that pushes back, then read a forensic debrief on
> exactly where they lost the room. It is AI roleplay for real estate agents — a safe
> place to take the reps that otherwise cost real listings and real commissions.

## What it is
The Walkthrough turns a high-stakes real estate conversation into a low-risk rehearsal.
You paste in the deal (a listing, a buyer brief, a seller's last text), and the app builds
an AI persona around it — a seller defending their Zillow price, a buyer questioning your
fee, an FSBO who insists they don't need an agent. You have the hard conversation out loud,
with no commission on the line. After every rep, a forensic debrief shows the exact moment
your value framing slipped, your commission defense wobbled, or an objection got past you.

## Who it's for
Licensed real estate agents and aspiring agents — especially new agents preparing for a
first listing, and anyone who needs to rehearse a listing presentation, an objection, a
FSBO or expired-listing call, or a negotiation before it's real money. Also sold to teams
and brokerages as a white-label training rail.

## Core mechanic
Set the room → run the rep against an AI client that pushes back → read the forensic
debrief → fix one thing → run it again. Purpose-built scenario rooms include: the listing
pitch, "your commission is too high," the lowball offer, the FSBO call, the expired listing,
and the inspection renegotiation.

## Key facts (evidence-based)
- The top five objections account for 74% of all objections across 300M+ calls (Gong Labs, 2023) — a small, trainable set decides most rooms.
- Sales forces that rate their training highly win 58% of deals versus 47% for weaker-trained teams (RAIN Group, 2023).
- Deliberate, feedback-driven practice cut performance errors by 60% versus conventional training (Annals of Surgery, 2021).
- 49% of the 2022 new-agent cohort failed to close a single deal the following year (Relitix, 2024).
- Median income: $92,500 for agents with 16+ years versus $8,100 for those with 2 years or less (NAR 2024 Member Profile).
- Around $21,000 in commission rides on a single median-priced home (Clever Real Estate, 2026).
Every claim on the site maps to a published source on the research page.

## Pricing
- Solo (Individual Agent): $9.99 / month — 30 reps and drills a month; seller, buyer, FSBO, and counter-offer personas; forensic debrief on every session; cancel anytime in Apple Account.
- Brokerages & teams: white-label, custom pricing — contact contact@hldelaney.com.
The app is iOS-only and launching soon (not yet on the App Store). No ads, no in-app upsells.

## Privacy
Voice input is transcribed to text and the raw audio is discarded — no voice recordings are
stored. Drill content is not used to train third-party AI models and is never sold.

## Maker
Built by H.L. Delaney, the studio behind Rehearsal Room AI.
